result.stdout:
dmr-client
  Microsoft IoT Models Repository CommandLine v1.0.0-beta.9+10ea252fd3f61aeea906d6e2a35ca169f3bf22d3

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] [command]

Options:
  --debug         Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent        Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  export    Exports a model producing the model and its dependency chain in an expanded format. The target repository is used for model resolution.
  validate  Validates the DTDL model contained in a file. When validating a single model object the target repository is used for model resolution. When validating an array of models only the array contents is used for resolution.
  import    Imports models from a model file into the local repository. The local repository is used for model resolution. Target model files for import will first be validated to ensure adherence to IoT Models Repository conventions.
  index     Builds a model index file from the state of a target local models repository.
  expand    For each model in a local repository, generate expanded model files and insert them in-place. The expanded version of a model includes the model with its full model dependency chain.

dmr-client

Usage:

Options:
  --debug         Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent        Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  export    Exports a model producing the model and its dependency chain in an expanded format. The target repository is used for model resolution.
  validate  Validates the DTDL model contained in a file. When validating a single model object the target repository is used for model resolution. When validating an array of models only the array contents is used for resolution.
  import    Imports models from a model file into the local repository. The local repository is used for model resolution. Target model files for import will first be validated to ensure adherence to IoT Models Repository conventions.
  index     Builds a model index file from the state of a target local models repository.
  expand    For each model in a local repository, generate expanded model files and insert them in-place. The expanded version of a model includes the model with its full model dependency chain.

result.stdout:
expand
  For each model in a local repository, generate expanded model files and insert them in-place. The expanded version of a model includes the model with its full model dependency chain.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] expand

Options:
  -r, --local-repo <local-repo>  Local models repository path. If no path is provided the current working directory is used. [default: ]
  --debug                        Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                       Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
expand
  For each model in a local repository, generate expanded model files and insert them in-place. The expanded version of a model includes the model with its full model dependency chain.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] expand

Options:
  -r, --local-repo <local-repo>  Local models repository path. If no path is provided the current working directory is used. [default: ]
  --debug                        Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                       Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
export
  Exports a model producing the model and its dependency chain in an expanded format. The target repository is used for model resolution.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] export

Options:
  --dtmi <dtmi>                    Digital Twin Model Identifier. Example: "dtmi:com:example:Thermostat;1".
  -m, --model-file <model-file>    Path to file containing Digital Twins model content.
  -r, --repo <repo>                Models repository location. Supports remote endpoint or local directory. [default: https://devicemodels.azure.com/]
  -o, --output-file <output-file>  Desired file path to write result contents. [default: ]
  --debug                          Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                         Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
export
  Exports a model producing the model and its dependency chain in an expanded format. The target repository is used for model resolution.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] export

Options:
  --dtmi <dtmi>                    Digital Twin Model Identifier. Example: "dtmi:com:example:Thermostat;1".
  -m, --model-file <model-file>    Path to file containing Digital Twins model content.
  -r, --repo <repo>                Models repository location. Supports remote endpoint or local directory. [default: https://devicemodels.azure.com/]
  -o, --output-file <output-file>  Desired file path to write result contents. [default: ]
  --debug                          Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                         Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
import
  Imports models from a model file into the local repository. The local repository is used for model resolution. Target model files for import will first be validated to ensure adherence to IoT Models Repository conventions.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] import

Options:
  -m, --model-file <model-file>          Path to file containing Digital Twins model content.
  --directory <directory>                Path to directory containing Digital Twins model content.
  --search-pattern <search-pattern>      The search string to match against the names of files in directory path. This parameter can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters, but it doesn't support regular expressions. [default: *.json]
  -r, --local-repo <local-repo>          Local models repository path. If no path is provided the current working directory is used. [default: ]
  --force                                Forces import operation to override existing model file. [default: False]
  --max-dtdl-version <max-dtdl-version>  Sets the maximum DTDL version accepted. [default: 2]
  --debug                                Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                               Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
import
  Imports models from a model file into the local repository. The local repository is used for model resolution. Target model files for import will first be validated to ensure adherence to IoT Models Repository conventions.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] import

Options:
  -m, --model-file <model-file>          Path to file containing Digital Twins model content.
  --directory <directory>                Path to directory containing Digital Twins model content.
  --search-pattern <search-pattern>      The search string to match against the names of files in directory path. This parameter can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters, but it doesn't support regular expressions. [default: *.json]
  -r, --local-repo <local-repo>          Local models repository path. If no path is provided the current working directory is used. [default: ]
  --force                                Forces import operation to override existing model file. [default: False]
  --max-dtdl-version <max-dtdl-version>  Sets the maximum DTDL version accepted. [default: 2]
  --debug                                Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                               Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
index
  Builds a model index file from the state of a target local models repository.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] index

Options:
  -r, --local-repo <local-repo>               Local models repository path. If no path is provided the current working directory is used. [default: ]
  -o, --output-file <output-file> (REQUIRED)  File path to write root model index content. Index pages will be placed relative to this path. [default: index.json]
  --page-limit <page-limit>                   Maximum models per page. [default: 2048]
  --debug                                     Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                                    Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
index
  Builds a model index file from the state of a target local models repository.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] index

Options:
  -r, --local-repo <local-repo>               Local models repository path. If no path is provided the current working directory is used. [default: ]
  -o, --output-file <output-file> (REQUIRED)  File path to write root model index content. Index pages will be placed relative to this path. [default: index.json]
  --page-limit <page-limit>                   Maximum models per page. [default: 2048]
  --debug                                     Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                                    Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
validate
  Validates the DTDL model contained in a file. When validating a single model object the target repository is used for model resolution. When validating an array of models only the array contents is used for resolution.

Usage:
  dmr-client [options] validate

Options:
  -m, --model-file <model-file>          Path to file containing Digital Twins model content.
  --directory <directory>                Path to directory containing Digital Twins model content.
  --search-pattern <search-pattern>      The search string to match against the names of files in directory path. This parameter can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters, but it doesn't support regular expressions. [default: *.json]
  -r, --repo <repo>                      Models repository location. Supports remote endpoint or local directory. [default: https://devicemodels.azure.com/]
  --strict                               Runs additional verifications for a model including file paths and DTMI scoping. [default: False]
  --max-dtdl-version <max-dtdl-version>  Sets the maximum DTDL version accepted. [default: 2]
  --debug                                Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]
  --silent                               Silences command output on standard out. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                         Show help and usage information

  --debug                                Shows additional logs for debugging. [default: False]

  -?, -h, --help                         Show help and usage information
